manual_entries.title: ASHRAE AHU System Mode Hierarchy
manual_entries.summary: AHU system mode follows the highest-priority zone group mode in a defined hierarchy.

structured_payload.guidance: When zone groups served by an air-handling system are in different modes, the AHU mode follows the highest-priority hierarchy: Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, Unoccupied.

doc.file_name: ASHRAE Guideline 36 High-Performance Sequences of Operation for HVAC Systems.pdf

chunk.cleaned_text: AHU system mode follows the highest-priority zone group mode hierarchy: Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, Unoccupied.
chunk.text_excerpt: AHU system mode hierarchy occupied cooldown setup warmup setback unoccupied

evidence.evidence_text: AHU system modes are the same as the mode of the Zone Group served by the system. When Zone Group served by an air-handling system are in different modes, the following hierarchy applies: Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, Unoccupied.

source_manual.path: /Volumes/TSD302/19.暖通论文/英文文献/手册与标准/HVAC系统高性能运行序列指南.pdf
